symbols: Mary enthroned with the child — the Seat of Wisdom and Queen of Heaven
Saint George in armor — the defender of the faith and conqueror of evil
infant John the Baptist — the forerunner recognizing Christ even in childhood
putti playing below — heavenly joy and innocence surrounding the holy gathering
fruit garland above — abundance and the fruits of salvation
classical architecture — the grandeur and order of the heavenly court
description: The Virgin Mary sits enthroned at center beneath an ornate classical arch decorated with a fruit garland. She holds the infant Jesus who reaches toward a figure below. To the left, a muscular male figure (possibly a saint) gestures upward. To the right, a young knight in armor and a red cape (Saint George) stands holding a banner or staff. Below the throne, playful putti and a small child (possibly the infant John the Baptist) are grouped. The background shows a landscape through the arch. The composition is warm and richly colored.
theological_description: Correggio's Madonna with Saint George is a sacra conversazione — a devotional gathering of the Virgin and Child with saints across time. Mary enthroned with the Christ child embodies the theological truth of the Incarnation: 'The Word became flesh and dwelt among us, and we have seen his glory' (John 1:14). Saint George, the legendary dragon-slayer and patron of soldiers, represents the spiritual warfare Paul describes: 'Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil' (Ephesians 6:11). The infant John the Baptist's presence recalls the connection between the two families — when Mary visited Elizabeth, 'the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit' (Luke 1:41). The playful putti embody the heavenly joy that Jesus said belongs to children: 'Let the little children come to me and do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of heaven' (Matthew 19:14). The abundant fruit garland over the throne echoes Jesus's promise: 'I came that they may have life and have it abundantly' (John 10:10). The entire composition presents the Church as a community gathered around the incarnate Lord.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word made flesh dwelling among us — the enthroned Christ child as the visible presence of God
The armor of God — Saint George in full armor representing the Church Militant
The infant John leaping in recognition of Christ — the child Baptist present before the Christ child
Let the children come to me — the joyful putti gathered around the throne of Christ
Christ bringing abundant life — the fruit garland symbolizing the abundance of salvation
